description_long: This research institute is a large, multi-story building with a complex footprint, featuring a combination of brick and glass facades. The structure has a prominent curved roof section, likely made of metal, that covers the central portion of the building, while flat and angular rooflines define the adjoining wings. Large vertical windows dominate the front and side elevations, providing ample natural light to the interior spaces. The main entrance is accentuated by a tall glass atrium framed by brick columns and a gabled section with additional windows. The building's exterior is primarily composed of reddish-brown brick, light gray metal or concrete, and extensive glass panels. The overall design suggests a contemporary institutional style with functional and aesthetic elements suited for academic or research purposes.
description: A large, contemporary research institute with a curved metal roof, brick and glass facades, and a prominent glass atrium entrance.
description_view_0: This view highlights the curved roof and the brick and glass combination, with the main entrance partially visible on the right.
description_view_1: The front facade is shown, featuring the tall glass atrium, brick columns, and the main entryway with large windows.
description_view_2: This side view displays the gabled section, additional windows, and the continuation of the curved roof over the central area.
description_view_3: The rear and side of the building are visible, showing more brickwork, a series of smaller windows, and the extended flat roof sections.
attribute_entryway_design: The main entryway is a tall, wide glass atrium framed by brick columns, featuring large transparent doors and a gabled section above. The entrance is at ground level, likely accessible, and is visually prominent with extensive glazing and minimal decorative elements.
